_comment_: Network panel copy. Currently unused (info panel removed in Phase A); kept for when a panel returns or for the cross-paper compare route.

whatItDoes: Holds task goals in mind, plans action, monitors performance, suppresses distraction.
whatLightsUp: Bilateral dorsolateral prefrontal cortex and inferior parietal lobule.

whatItDoes: Active during rest, mind-wandering, autobiographical memory, mentalizing about other minds.
whatLightsUp: Medial prefrontal cortex, precuneus, posterior cingulate, bilateral angular gyrus.

whatItDoes: Detects what matters in the moment; gates which network gets resources by switching between CEN and DMN.
whatLightsUp: Bilateral anterior insula and dorsal anterior cingulate.

deltas.cen->dmn: From outward, goal-driven control to inward, self-referential thought — the brain shifts from doing to reflecting.
deltas.dmn->cen: From inward reflection back to outward task focus.
deltas.cen->sn: From sustained executive control to the moment-to-moment salience detector that decides what deserves attention.
deltas.sn->cen: From salience-gating back into executive control of the chosen task.
deltas.dmn->sn: From internal narrative to the salience network — the gatekeeper that determines whether to stay in mind-wandering or switch out.
deltas.sn->dmn: From salience detection back into self-referential thought.

overlaps.cen+dmn: CEN and DMN are typically anticorrelated — when one is active, the other tends to quiet. Their parcels live in different cortical territory: CEN lateral, DMN medial.
overlaps.cen+sn: CEN and SN both involve frontal cortex but in different territories — SN sits in the anterior insula and ACC, CEN in dlPFC.
overlaps.dmn+sn: SN modulates DMN: the salience network can suppress DMN activity when external attention is required.
overlaps.cen+dmn+sn: Menon's triple-network model: SN switches between CEN (external focus) and DMN (internal focus) depending on what's salient.
